blocks.statement: I can apply all of Unit 1's tools — vectors, F = ma, free-body diagrams, friction, equilibrium — to the asteroid scenario in one integrated analysis.

blocks.connection: Today the whole toolkit comes together on 2026-XJ. This is the dress rehearsal for the transfer task: one coherent picture of the asteroid through every Unit 1 lens.

blocks.markdown:
## The whole unit, one object

Walk through 2026-XJ using everything you have learned:

- **Free-body diagram** in deep space: solar gravity dominates; Earth and planetary gravity are weaker; there is **no friction**.
- **Net force** is essentially solar gravity, pointing toward the Sun.
- **F = ma** → the asteroid accelerates toward the Sun, which bends its path.
- **Velocity** is 8,500 m/s at 15° toward Earth — decompose it into a toward-Earth part and a sideways part.
- **Predict** its position months out using the constant-velocity approximation, and state the **uncertainty**.

blocks.title: 2026-XJ velocity, decomposed
blocks.caption: The single velocity arrow (8,500 m/s, 15° toward Earth) splits into a toward-Earth component and a perpendicular component.

blocks.instruction: Draw the free-body diagram for 2026-XJ in deep space. Put the asteroid at the center dot and draw one arrow per force. Remember: out there, there is no friction and no normal force — gravity is the whole story.
prompts: Solar gravity: a long arrow toward the Sun (the dominant force).
Earth's gravity: a short arrow toward Earth.
No friction, no normal force in deep space.
Label each arrow and circle the one that sets the net force.

blocks.prompt: 2026-XJ travels at 8,500 m/s aimed 15° toward Earth. Find its toward-Earth (horizontal) velocity component. Then state how you would predict its position 30 days later, and why that prediction has a window of uncertainty.
blocks.equationHint: Toward-Earth component = v · cos(15°); then displacement = v · t.

blocks.frame: The one tool I most need ready for the transfer task is ___ because ___.
blocks.prompt: What is the one tool from this unit you most need to have ready for the transfer task, and why?
